rulings.Case Title: LEGAL RECOVERY SERVICES VS. CRYSTAL LEI ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 17, 2021, line 1. DEFENDANT BRYANT FU, TING POON, CRYSTAL LEI's DEMURRER to 4TH Amended COMPLAINT. sustained without leave to amend as to the cause of action for fraudulent conveyances. By June 23, 2021 ruling Plaintiff was instructed to allege facts regarding non-time barred fraudulent conveyances as to each defendant. Fourth Amended Complaint alleges specific facts as to time barred-conveyances the last of which occurred in 2006. Fourth Amended Complaint makes conclusory statements as to "present" date conveyances in the form of rent (no factual allegations as to the rental property actually generating income in the last 7 years) and amounts in the construction company bank account (no factual allegations that any amounts in the bank accounts were judgment debtor's earnings or income to which judgment debtor is entitled). Applying authorities cited by Plaintiff for the accrual of the cause of action as of the time of the "last over act" in furtherance of the conspiracy, the last properly allege factual assertion of such act dates to 2006, i.e. outside of the statute of limitations. Furthermore, Plaintiff appears to seek re-adjudication of the judgment debtor's assets and earnings between 2003 and 2019. Debtor obtained a discharge in 2019 by representing to the bankruptcy Court that he had no assets and no earnings. Plaintiff is now asking this Court to come to a different determination and find that debtor had assets in his sister's/son's, etc., name during the same time. Any determination as to the fraudulently obtained bankruptcy discharge (including the determination of any of the debtor's assets held by other people) should have been or should be addressed in the bankruptcy Court. The narrow issue that is to be addressed by this court is the existence of any community property shares belonging to the judgment debtor. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: TRINITY MANAGEMENT SERVICES, INC VS. MORIAH NAKADA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 17, 2021, line 3. DEFENDANT MORIAH NAKADA's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint and notice sufficiently state a cause of action for unlawful detainer based on interference with "comfortable enjoyment of life and property" and safety at the building. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
